schools.summary: Gill: 'after many days,' that is, years.
Keil & Delitzsch: Joshua had withdrawn to Timnath-serah to spend his last days.

themes: a long time after that the LORD had given rest unto Israel... Joshua waxed old and stricken in age
the faithful servant near his end, the land at rest
we spend our years as a tale that is told (Ps. 90:9)

schools.summary: Gill: he summoned them, whether at Timnath-serah, Shechem, or Shiloh; addressed the elders, heads, judges, and officers.

themes: Joshua called for all Israel, and for their elders... judges... officers... I am old and stricken in age
the godly leaving counsel and testimony to those who lead after them
the things thou hast heard of me... commit thou to faithful men (2 Tim. 2:2)

schools.summary: Gill: how the Lord had destroyed and driven out the seven nations of Canaan to make room for them; the LORD your God is he that hath fought for you.

themes: ye have seen all that the LORD your God hath done... for the LORD your God is he that hath fought for you
all their victories God's doing, not their own swords
the LORD shall fight for you, and ye shall hold your peace (Exod. 14:14)

schools.summary: Gill: he divided to them by lot the nations that remained, unsubdued, as well as those conquered.
Keil & Delitzsch: the nations that remain given as an inheritance.

themes: I have divided unto you by lot these nations that remain, to be an inheritance... with all the nations that I have cut off
God's gift sure though not yet fully enjoyed
the LORD will give grace and glory: no good thing will he withhold (Ps. 84:11)

schools.summary: Gill: the Lord would expel such of them as yet remained among them.
Keil & Delitzsch: the LORD driving them out and Israel possessing their land.

themes: the LORD your God, he shall expel them from before you... and ye shall possess their land, as... he hath promised
the God who begins his people's deliverance finishing it
he which hath begun a good work in you will perform it (Phil. 1:6)

schools.summary: Gill: to observe the law required spiritual strength, fortitude, holy courage, and firm resolution; they were to turn not aside to the right hand or the left.

themes: Be ye therefore very courageous to keep and to do all that is written in the book of the law of Moses
faithfulness to God's word demanding a brave, resolute heart
this book of the law shall not depart out of thy mouth (Josh. 1:8)

schools.summary: Gill: they were not to converse freely or familiarly with the remaining nations, nor mention the name of their gods, nor swear by, serve, or bow to them.

themes: neither make mention of the name of their gods, nor... serve them, nor bow yourselves unto them
shunning every approach to idolatry, keeping unspotted
come out from among them, and be ye separate... touch not the unclean thing (2 Cor. 6:17)

schools.summary: Gill: to cleave to his word, worship, and ordinances externally, and internally by strong affection, hope, trust, and confidence in him.

themes: But cleave unto the LORD your God, as ye have done unto this day
the sum of true religion, holding fast to the LORD with the whole heart
that with purpose of heart they would cleave unto the Lord (Acts 11:23)

schools.summary: Gill: these nations were very numerous and populous, reckoned great and strong in those times; no man had been able to stand before Israel.

themes: the LORD hath driven out from before you great nations and strong: but... no man hath been able to stand before you
the people of God invincible with him
if God be for us, who can be against us? (Rom. 8:31)

schools.summary: Gill: some read the future for the past — one had chased a thousand; the LORD your God is he that fighteth for you, as he promised.

themes: One man of you shall chase a thousand: for the LORD your God, he it is that fighteth for you
the strength of God's people wholly in him
how should one chase a thousand... except the LORD had shut them up? (Deut. 32:30)

schools.summary: Gill: they needed to be on their guard and watchful, lest they be ensnared by the Canaanites and drawn into idolatry; they should love the LORD their God.

themes: Take good heed therefore unto yourselves, that ye love the LORD your God
love to God guarded by diligent self-watch
keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it are the issues of life (Prov. 4:23)

schools.summary: Gill: going back from the Lord and his worship, his word and ordinances.
Clarke: the soldier who draws back forfeits his life; these were the Lord's soldiers.

themes: if ye do in any wise go back, and cleave unto the remnant of these nations... and shall make marriages with them
unholy alliances the door of apostasy
be ye not unequally yoked together with unbelievers (2 Cor. 6:14)

schools.summary: Gill: 'knowing ye shall know' — the fullest assurance is given; they should be snares, traps, scourges in their sides, and thorns in their eyes.

themes: they shall be snares and traps unto you, and scourges in your sides, and thorns in your eyes
the spared enemy becoming a perpetual scourge
they shall be... thorns in your sides, and shall vex you in the land (Num. 33:55)

schools.summary: Gill: Joshua was about to die, going the way of all the earth; not one thing had failed of all the good things the LORD promised, all are come to pass.

themes: not one thing hath failed of all the good things which the LORD your God spake concerning you; all are come to pass
God performing every promise, not one good thing falling to the ground
there hath not failed one word of all his good promise (1 Kgs 8:56)

schools.summary: Gill: there was full proof that all the good things promised had come, and it could not be denied.
Keil & Delitzsch: the threatened bringing of all evil things.

themes: as all good things are come upon you... so shall the LORD bring upon you all evil things
God as faithful to his warnings as to his promises
the LORD... will not at all acquit the wicked (Nah. 1:3)

schools.summary: Gill: the transgressing of the covenant of the Lord.
Clarke: citing Trapp, this judgment Joshua inculcates again and again.

themes: When ye have transgressed the covenant... and have gone and served other gods... ye shall perish quickly from off the good land
apostasy the sure road to ruin, the good land no safeguard
if ye forsake him, he will forsake you (2 Chr. 15:2)
